questions.question: Explain the concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) under E-Waste Rules 2011.
keyPoints: EPR makes producers responsible for end-of-life management of their products
Producers must set up collection mechanisms for e-waste
Producers must ensure recycling through authorized dismantlers/recyclers
Producers must achieve collection targets specified by government

questions.question: List four hazardous substances present in e-waste and their effects on human health.
keyPoints: Lead - neurological damage, brain damage in children
Mercury - brain and nervous system damage

questions.question: Describe the role of Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) in e-waste management.
What are the major categories of e-waste under the 2011 Rules?
Explain the process of dismantling in e-waste management.
keyPoints: Separation of components into recyclable and non-recyclable
Recovery of valuable materials for recycling

questions.question: What are the environmental impacts of improper e-waste disposal?
Discuss the key provisions of E-Waste (Management and Handling) Rules 2011 and explain how they aim to manage e-waste in India.
Explain the various stakeholders in e-waste management and their responsibilities under the E-Waste Rules 2011.
keyPoints: Consumers - proper disposal at collection centers

questions.question: Describe the hazards associated with e-waste and explain why informal e-waste processing is harmful.
keyPoints: Health effects - respiratory, neurological, kidney damage
Informal processing exposes workers to toxins
Lack of safety equipment and protocols

questions.question: What measures can be adopted to improve e-waste management in India?
keyPoints: Strengthening infrastructure for collection and recycling
Integration of informal sector with formal system
